question: Can any asset sale proceeds be used to make dividends?

stage1_classifications.rationale: Builder basket permits dividends; asset sale proceeds feed into it.
General RP basket permits dividends but not asset-sale-proceeds specific.
Ratio basket permits dividends but not asset-sale-proceeds specific.
Equity repurchase only; unrelated to asset sale proceeds.
Tax distributions only; unrelated to asset sale proceeds.
Overhead payments only; unrelated to asset sale proceeds.
Equity repurchase on exercise; unrelated to asset sale proceeds.
Allows distributing unrestricted sub assets/proceeds as dividend.

IP transfer restriction; unrelated to asset sale proceeds for dividends.

stage2_citations.quote: "Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):
"Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):

additional Dividends may be made, directly or indirectly, by any Group Member to Holdings and/or (without duplication) Holdings' direct or indirect equity holders in an aggregate amount not to exceed the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA
unlimited additional Dividends; provided that on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio...shall be no greater than the greater of (x) 5.75 to 1.00

stage2_citations.section: Definition of Applicable Net Cash Proceeds Percentage, clause (a)
stage2_citations.quote: 100% if the First Lien Leverage Ratio...is greater than 5.75 to 1.00

stage2_answer: Yes, asset sale proceeds can be used to make dividends through multiple pathways. First, the retained (non-swept) Net Cash Proceeds of asset sales feed into the Cumulative Amount as an additive source [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32], and the builder basket explicitly permits dividends to be paid from that Cumulative Amount [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]; the amount retained (vs. swept) depends on the leverage-based sweep tiers — 0% sweep (full retention) if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.50x, 50% sweep if > 5.50x but ≤ 5.75x, and 100% sweep if > 5.75x [Definition of Applicable Net Cash Proceeds Percentage, clauses (a)–(c)]. Second, the general RP basket (up to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA) and the ratio basket (unlimited if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x pro forma, or no-worse test) permit dividends from any cash source, including asset sale proceeds, without a source-of-funds restriction [Section 6.06(j), p. 196; Section 6.06(o), p. 198].
stage2_reasoning_conclusion: Yes — asset sale proceeds can be used to make dividends through at least two pathways: (1) the retained (non-swept) portion of asset sale Net Cash Proceeds builds the Cumulative Amount under the builder basket, which permits dividends; and (2) the general RP basket and ratio basket permit dividends from any source of funds up to their respective caps or leverage conditions.
stage2_reasoning_analysis: The key mechanism is the builder basket (Cumulative Amount). Norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds is a source component (child_index 6, additive) that feeds into the Cumulative Amount (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission). The proceeds_flow edge confirms that Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales subject to mandatory prepayment under Section 2.10(c) flow into clause (f) of the Cumulative Amount when retained — specifically, the portion NOT required to be swept under the leverage-based tier mechanics. The sweep tier structure governs how much must be prepaid versus retained: (a) 100% sweep if First Lien Leverage Ratio > 5.75x; (b) 50% sweep if > 5.50x but ≤ 5.75x; (c) 0% sweep (i.e., full retention) if ≤ 5.50x. Only the retained (non-swept) portion of asset sale proceeds feeds the Cumulative Amount. Once in the Cumulative Amount, those proceeds can be used to make dividends under the builder basket permission (norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission), which explicitly scopes make_dividend_payment. Separately, dividends can also be funded via the general RP basket (Section 6.06(j), up to the greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA) or the ratio basket (Section 6.06(o), unlimited if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x pro forma). Those baskets are not limited to any particular source of funds — they are capacity-based, so any cash including asset sale proceeds could fund dividends drawn under those baskets. There are no defeaters in the data restricting use of asset sale proceeds for dividends specifically.
